knowledge.html:
1. Loi moi va cach dap lai loi moi

Tinh huong mauDong yTu choi
- Would you like a cup of coffee?
- Would you like to come to my party this Saturday?
- Would you care to join us?
- Do you want to go out with me tonight?
- Do you feel like going for a walk?		- Yes, please.
- I'd love to, thanks.
- That's very kind of you, thanks.
- It's very nice of you, thanks.
- That sounds lovely, thanks.
- Thank you for your kind invitation.
- I'll be glad to do so.
- Thanks, I'd like that very much.
- That's a great idea.
- Thanks for inviting me.
- Many thanks for your kind invitation. I'll join you.
- With pleasure!
- Sure. / Yeah, why not!
- Sounds good.		- I'm sorry to refuse your invitation.
- I can't, sorry. I have to work.
- Thanks for your invitation but I'm busy now.
- I'm afraid I won't be able to come.
- I'm afraid I am busy tomorrow.
- Sorry, I'd love to but I have an appointment.
- I really don't think I can, sorry.
- That's very kind of you, but I can't accept your invitation.

2. Loi yeu cau, de nghi va cach dap lai

2.1. Hoi xin phep lam gi va cach dap lai

Tinh huong mauDong yTu choi
- Can I borrow your books?
- Could I have some cake?
- Could I possibly sit here?
- Is it OK/all right if I open the window?		- Yes, sure. / Yes, of course.
- Yes, that's fine.
- Certainly.
- Of course, you can.		- Well, I'm afraid... (+ ly do)
- Well, the problem is...
- Do you mind if I turn on the TV?- No, not at all.
- No, of course not.		- Sorry, but...
- May I help you?- Yes, please.- Thank you. I'll do it myself.
- Can I have the bill?- Just a moment/minute.
- Can I bring my friends to the party?- The more the merrier.

2.2. Loi de nghi, yeu cau va cach dap lai

Tinh huong mauDong yTu choi
- Can you help me with this exercise?
- Could you close the door?
- Will you help me give this letter to him?
- Would you make dinner today?
- Do you mind turning off the lights before going out?
- Would you mind cleaning the house?		- Yes, sure.
- Yes, of course.
- Certainly.
- No, not at all.
- Of course not.		- Well, I'm afraid + (ly do)
- Well, the problem is...
- Sorry, but... (Xin loi, nhung...)

3. Loi goi y va cach dap lai

Tinh huong mauDong yTu choi
- Let's go out for lunch.
- What about going to the beach this summer?
- How about cooking at home?
- Why don't we eat some fruit now?
- Couldn't we go to the park?
- Shall we go by train?
- Does it matter if we leave a bit earlier?		- Yes, I'd love to.
- What a good idea!
- Why not?
- Yes, that sounds like a great idea.
- Yes, that's not a bad idea.
- Count me in too.
- Yes, let's.
- It sounds good to me.
- I'm up for it.
- Let's do that.
- I can't agree more.		- No, let's not.
- Well, I'd rather... I prefer...
- I don't feel like it.
- No, thanks.
- I'm not sure.
- I don't think that's a good idea.
- We had better not...
- We had better/we should...

4. Loi xin loi va cach dap lai

Tinh huong mauChap nhan loi xin loi
Sorry, I'm late.
(Xin loi minh den muon.)		- It doesn't matter.
- Don't apologize.
- That's all right.
- It's alright.
- It's okay.
- Don't mention it.
- Never mind.
- No worries.
- I quite understand.

5. Loi cam on va cach dap lai

Tinh huong mauCach dap lai
Thank you for helping me.- That's all right!
- You're welcome.
- Don't mention it.
- Not at all.
- It's nothing.
- My pleasure.

6. Khi dua y kien va cach dap lai

Dong yDong y mot phanPhan doi
- I completely/absolutely agree with you.
- There is no doubt about it that...
- I can't/couldn't agree more.
- I completely agree.
- That's so true.
- Absolutely.
- Exactly.
- Of course.
- You're absolutely right.
- Yes, I agree.
- I think so too.
- That's a good idea.
- I don't think so either.
- So do I. / Neither do I.
- I'd go along with that.
- That's true.
- I agree with you entirely.
- That's just what I was thinking.
- You can say that again!		- I agree up to a point, but...
- That's true but...
- You could be right.
- It sounds interesting, but...
- I see your point, but...
- That's partly true, but...
- I can agree with that only with reservations.
- That seems obvious, but...
- That is not necessarily so.
- It is not as simple as it seems.
- I agree with you in principle, but...
- I agree with you in part, but...
- Well, you could be right.		- I totally disagree.
- I don't think so!
- No way.
- I'm afraid, I can't agree with you.
- I don't agree with you.
- To be honest...
- On the contrary,...
- I'm sorry, but I disagree.
- It's out of question.
- That's different.
- However,...
- That's not entirely true.
- Yes, but don't you think...
- I'm not so sure about that.
- The problem is that...
- I (very much) doubt whether...

7. Mot so tinh huong khac

Tinh huongCach dap lai
Gap ai do lan dau tien:
Hello. Nice to meet you!		Nice/Glad to meet you, too.
How do you do?
Gap do va chuc:
Have a nice day!		You too.
The same to you.
Thank you, the same to you.
You do the same!
Khen/chuc mung:
What a nice car!
You look so lovely!		I'm glad you like it.
I'm glad you think so.
Thank you.
It's (very) nice of you to say so.
Thank you (very much) for saying so.
Chuc mung tin vui:
I've passed my driving exam.		That's great. Congratulations!
Truoc khi an:
Bon appetite!		Bon appetite!
Enjoy your meal!
Nho dua vat gi:
Could you please pass me the salt?		Here you are!
Duoc tang qua:That's very kind (nice/thoughtful) of you!
Nguoi ban hang hoi:
Do you need anything else?		That's all. Thank you!
Hoi suc khoe:
How are you?		Rat tot: Very well, thanks. / Pretty fair. / I'm on the top of the world. / Can't complain.
Binh thuong: I'm fine/good/great, thanks. / I'm alright.
Khong tot: Really bad. / I'm not on a good mood.
Phan nan:
Khi ai do phan nan ve dieu gi		Tich cuc: I'm so sorry, but this will never occur/happen again. / I'm sorry; we'll do our utmost/best not to do the same mistake again.
Tieu cuc: Sorry, there is nothing we can do about it. / I'm afraid, there isn't much we can do about it.

exercises.question:
Hung: "I think we should save energy at home as well as at school."
Lan: "_______"
exercises.explanation: Hung dua ra y kien/goi y. Cach dong y tu nhien nhat la 'That's a good idea'.

exercises.question:
Hoa: "Wow! What a nice coat you are wearing!"
Mai: "_______"
options: A. Certainly. Do you like it, too?
B. I like you to say that.
C. Yes, of course. It's expensive.
D. Thanks. My mother bought it for me.
exercises.explanation: Khi duoc khen, cach dap tu nhien la cam on. 'Thanks. My mother bought it for me.'

exercises.question:
A: "I'm taking my first exam next week."
B: "_______"
exercises.explanation: Nguoi A sap thi, nen chuc 'Good luck' (Chuc may man). 'Congratulations' chi dung khi da thanh cong.

exercises.question:
A: "Would you mind if I smoke here?"
B: "_______"
exercises.explanation: 'I'd rather you didn't' = toi muon ban dung lam vay. Day la cach tu choi lich su voi 'Would you mind...?'

exercises.question:
Minh: "Personally, I love peaceful and quiet life in rural areas."
Phong: "_______"
exercises.explanation: 'So do I' = toi cung vay. Dung de dong y voi cau khang dinh cua nguoi khac.

exercises.question:
A: "How about going to Paris for our next holiday?"
B: "_______"
exercises.explanation: 'How about...?' la loi goi y. 'Sounds great!' la cach dong y nhiet tinh.

exercises.question:
Nga: "We've passed the first round."
Hoa: "_______"
options: B. It's nice of you to say so.
exercises.explanation: Khi nghe tin vui (da vuot qua vong dau), dap lai bang 'Congratulations!' (Chuc mung!).

exercises.question:
A: "It was very kind of you to invite me to dinner."
B: "_______"
exercises.explanation: Khi duoc cam on, 'It's my pleasure' (Vinh hanh cua toi) la cach dap lich su.

exercises.question:
Nam: "I don't go out much these days."
Giang: "_______"
exercises.explanation: 'Neither do I' = toi cung khong. Dung de dong y voi cau phu dinh.

exercises.question:
Linh: "I'm afraid I failed my driving test."
Trinh: "_______"
options: B. Never mind. Better luck next time!
C. Oh dear. I don't think I can.
D. Oh, I think you shouldn't.
exercises.explanation: Khi nghe tin buon, 'Never mind. Better luck next time!' la cach an ui tu nhien.
